name: Storybook
what_it_does: UI component development environment and documentation tool. Lets developers build, test, and document components in isolation. Chromatic (the commercial product from Storybook maintainers) adds visual regression testing and a hosted component review platform with a REST API.
use_cases: Documenting UI components in isolation with interactive controls
Visual regression testing via Chromatic's API integration in CI
Design system component libraries with living documentation
Accessibility testing via addon-a11y during component development
Triggering Chromatic builds and checking visual diff status via REST API
not_for: End-to-end application testing (use Playwright or Cypress)
Teams without a component-based frontend architecture
best_when: An agent is managing a design system or component library and needs to trigger visual regression checks or query component documentation status via Chromatic's API.
avoid_when: You don't have a component-based UI architecture, or you need functional rather than visual testing.

auth.notes: Storybook OSS requires no authentication. Chromatic uses a project token (CHROMATIC_PROJECT_TOKEN) for CI integration. Tokens are project-scoped and set as CI environment variables.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Storybook OSS is fully free. Chromatic free tier: 5,000 snapshots/month.
pricing.paid_tiers: Chromatic from $149/mo (35,000 snapshots), Team plan $649/mo, Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Storybook OSS has no cost. Chromatic snapshot pricing can escalate quickly for large component libraries with many story variants. Free tier is limited for production design systems.

performance.rate_limits: N/A - local tool for Storybook OSS

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Storybook build errors can be difficult to diagnose because they mix webpack/Vite errors with story-level errors. Chromatic CLI output is cleaner and provides build URLs. Error messages from misconfigured stories often point to the wrong root cause.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Chromatic builds are uniquely identified per commit/branch. Re-running the same build may create a new Chromatic build rather than returning the existing one. Chromatic does deduplicate snapshots for unchanged stories.
known_agent_gotchas: Storybook configuration (main.js/.ts) syntax has changed significantly between major versions (v6, v7, v8)
CHROMATIC_PROJECT_TOKEN must be set in CI; missing token produces unhelpful auth errors
Storybook requires a correctly configured framework adapter (React, Vue, etc.) - auto-detection fails in monorepos
Story file naming conventions (*.stories.tsx) must match the configured glob pattern
Chromatic snapshot counts escalate rapidly with argTypes and complex story matrices
Chromatic's visual diff approval workflow is human-driven - agents can trigger builds but approval requires human review

score_rationale: Storybook itself is a local development tool with no meaningful REST API for agent use. Chromatic adds a cloud API surface for visual regression CI integration, but the API documentation is limited and the core workflow (approving visual diffs) inherently requires human review. Agent-readiness is further limited by complex configuration that varies by framework and Storybook version, and snapshot costs that escalate quickly. Best used as a tool agents can trigger in CI rather than one they orchestrate deeply.

security_score_components.security_notes: UI component development tool. Local dev tool — no external auth. Chromatic (cloud) uses project tokens. Never expose Storybook without auth in production.
